source_paper_title: Invariant States of Hydrodynamic Limits of Randomized Load-Balancing Networks
context:
Page 3, Section 1.1 (Introduction, discussion of equilibrium and future work)

Since the N-server stationary distributions are known to be tight (due to the results in [5]), to provide a rigorous proof of this convergence it would suffice to show that the solution to the hydrodynamic equations converges to the invariant state characterized here for a large class of initial conditions. Whereas this is a non-trivial problem that is relegated to future work, it is encouraging that analogous results have been recently established for a related problem.
title: Global convergence of the hydrodynamic equations to the physical invariant state (and hence interchange of limits for SQ\(d\) networks)
latex:
Given an integer \(d\ge 2\) and a load \(\lambda\in(0,1)\), consider the \(\mathrm{SQ}(d)\) randomized load-balancing model with i.i.d. service times having cumulative distribution function \(G\) on \([0,\infty)\), density \(g\), and mean \(\int_0^\infty \bar G(x)\,dx=1\), where \(\bar G(x)=1-G(x)\). Let \(L:=\sup\{x\ge 0:\bar G(x)>0\}\in(0,\infty]\) and define the hazard rate \(h(x):=g(x)/\bar G(x)\) for \(x\in[0,L)\).

Let \(\mathcal M_{\le 1}[0,L)\) be the set of sub-probability measures on \([0,L)\). Define the state space \(\mathcal S\) to be the set of sequences \(\nu=(\nu_\ell)_{\ell\ge 1}\) with \(\nu_\ell\in \mathcal M_{\le 1}[0,L)\) such that for every bounded continuous \(f\ge 0\) on \([0,\infty)\), \(\langle f,\nu_\ell\rangle \ge \langle f,\nu_{\ell+1}\rangle\). For \(x\ge y\ge 0\), define \(P_d(x,y):=(x^d-y^d)/(x-y)=\sum_{m=0}^{d-1} x^m y^{d-1-m}\), and let \(\delta_0\) denote the unit point mass at \(0\).

For an initial condition \(\nu(0)\in\mathcal S\), the hydrodynamic equations are the deterministic evolution \(t\mapsto \nu(t)=(\nu_\ell(t))_{\ell\ge 1}\in \mathcal S\) satisfying, for every \(\ell\ge 1\),
\[
D_\ell(t):=\int_0^t \langle h,\nu_\ell(s)\rangle\,ds,
\]
\[
\eta_1(t):=\lambda\bigl(1-\langle 1,\nu_1(t)\rangle^d\bigr)\,\delta_0,
\qquad
\eta_\ell(t):=\lambda\,P_d\bigl(\langle 1,\nu_{\ell-1}(t)\rangle,\langle 1,\nu_\ell(t)\rangle\bigr)\,\bigl(\nu_{\ell-1}(t)-\nu_\ell(t)\bigr)\ \ (\ell\ge 2),
\]
and for every bounded continuous \(f\) on \([0,\infty)\) and every \(t\ge 0\),
\[
\langle f,\nu_\ell(t)\rangle
=
\left\langle \frac{f(\cdot+t)\,\bar G(\cdot+t)}{\bar G(\cdot)},\nu_\ell(0)\right\rangle
+
\int_{[0,t]} f(t-s)\,\bar G(t-s)\,dD_{\ell+1}(s)
+
\int_0^t \left\langle \frac{f(\cdot+t-s)\,\bar G(\cdot+t-s)}{\bar G(\cdot)},\eta_\ell(s)\right\rangle ds.
\]
A state \(\nu^*\in\mathcal S\) is a physical invariant state if it is an invariant state (i.e., \(\nu(t)\equiv \nu^*\) solves the hydrodynamic equations) and its associated queue-length tail sequence \(s^*_\ell:=\langle 1,\nu^*_\ell\rangle\) satisfies \(\lim_{\ell\to\infty} s^*_\ell=0\).

Problem: establish global convergence to equilibrium for the hydrodynamic equations. More precisely, identify a broad class \(\mathcal I\subset\mathcal S\) of initial conditions (e.g., all \(\nu(0)\in\mathcal S\) with finite mean queue length \(\sum_{\ell\ge 1}\langle 1,\nu_\ell(0)\rangle<\infty\)) such that the unique solution \(\nu(t)\) to the hydrodynamic equations with \(\nu(0)\in\mathcal I\) satisfies
\[
\lim_{t\to\infty} \nu(t)=\nu^* \quad \text{in }\mathcal S.
\]
In particular, prove a quantitative or qualitative notion of global asymptotic stability of \(\nu^*\) for general service distributions \(G\) (beyond special classes such as decreasing hazard rate).

Motivation: such a result would imply, via tightness of the \(N\)-server stationary laws, that the stationary distributions of the \(N\)-server \(\mathrm{SQ}(d)\) systems converge as \(N\to\infty\) to the physical invariant state \(\nu^*\) (an interchange-of-limits/steady-state limit theorem).
